oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Beef cow-calf medicine for the dairy vet: A review of vaccinations, nutrition and performance assessments by season
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Clark, Kayla
oai_dc:dc.dc:subject: beef cow-calf
body condition scoring
mineral supplementation
vaccination
breeding soundness examination
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: Beef cow-calf medicine relies on consistent year-round man­agement supported by targeted seasonal interventions. Vet­erinarians are in a position to help producers integrate nutri­tion, mineral supplementation, vaccination protocols, and performance monitoring into practical systems that support long-term business success. This paper reviews foundational principles of beef cow-calf management from the perspec­tive of a dairy veterinarian working with beef producers in the Midwest. Emphasis is placed on year-round nutritional man­agement, mineral supplementation, vaccination strategies, fa­cility considerations, and seasonal performance benchmarks. A systems-based, seasonal approach allows veterinarians to better align recommendations with producer capabilities while improving reproductive efficiency, calf health and long-term herd productivity.
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: American Association of Bovine Practitioners Conference Proceedings; No. 1 (2026): 2026 Recent Graduate Conference Proceedings; 59-61
oai_dc:dc.dc:rights: Copyright (c) 2026 American Association of Bovine Practitioners
